Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

While this is a good start, I think as layouts get more complex that there is going to be a need for users/admins to copy file layouts more easily, rather than just putting this into test-framework.sh.

Patch Set 1:

In particular, I mean something like:

 lfs setstripe --copy=/path/to/oldfile /path/to/newfile

so that users can easily store and copy complex layouts without having to recreate them by hand each time.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

I think this patch is OK, but there needs to be second patch that adds a method for users to use to copy complex layouts to new files.  I don't think that will be difficult to implement.

If "--copy-layout" is given, start with a call to llapi_layout_get_by_path() to get the existing file's layout to populate the layout, they apply any other parameters from the command-line, and then finally llapi_layout_file_create() to create the new file.

commit.subject: LU-9324 test: get layout parameters
commit.message:
LU-9324 test: get layout parameters

Add test framework functions get_layout_param()/parse_layout_param
to get plain/PFL file/dir layout parameters, so that the layout
parameters can be used later.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-pfl
Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam.xu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I4f43a5b4d23e353660114b7ff299a97afc405a07
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/26578
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
